1.text: Braun & Clarke 6-Phase Thematic Analysis | v36 | 19 March 2026

text_boxes.text: Six critical PMF themes identified for Workday Recruiting in GCC

• Nationalization & Compliance: Primary blocker — Nitaqat 2026–2028 localises 340,000+ jobs; Emiratisation AED 108,000 per missing Emirati; no OOB GCC support
• Reporting & Dashboards: PowerBI/Excel workarounds; native dashboards insufficient
• Interview Scheduling: GCC populations cannot use Outlook/MS Teams; Paradox integration critical
• Offer Generation: 2-month config cycles; Arabic/RTL squares; candidate document upload missing
• WhatsApp: P1 'absolute necessary'; 98% open rate vs 20% email; GCC highest penetration
• Candidate Grid & Search: Multi-tab navigation; weak boolean search; AI-assisted matching desired

speaker_notes:
• Lead with Nationalization as #1 blocker.
• Emphasise Nitaqat 2026–2028 urgency.
• Reference customer quotes (P1, P2, P3).

References:
• Mondaq: Nitaqat 2026–2028
• Astute Analytica: GCC HR Tech Market

text_boxes.text: Research Challenge
Objectives and methodology

• How can Workday Recruiting better support hiring in the GCC region?
• Understand customer pain points, compliance requirements, and local preferences
• Identify product gaps and roadmap opportunities
• Triangulate customer interviews with Win/Loss data and PESTEL context

speaker_notes:
• Braun & Clarke 6-phase thematic analysis.
• 3 customer interviews, 2 GCC Win/Loss gaps.

References:
• Braun & Clarke (2006) Thematic Analysis

text_boxes.text:
Phase 0: Geographic filtering (GCC)
Phase 1: Familiarisation with data
Phase 2: Generating initial codes
Phase 3: Generating themes
Phase 4: Reviewing themes (triangulation)
Phase 5: Defining and naming themes
Phase 6: Producing report + slide deck

speaker_notes:
• Standard Braun & Clarke methodology.

References:
• Braun & Clarke (2006)

text_boxes.text: Context Review
Strategic context and market momentum

• GCC HR Tech: US$2.6B (2023) → US$5.5B (2032), CAGR 9.05%
• 70% of GCC companies plan to invest in HR tech
• Vision 2030 (Saudi) and economic diversification drive digital transformation
• Nationalization mandates (Nitaqat, Emiratisation) accelerate ATS adoption

speaker_notes:
• Astute Analytica market data.

References:
• https://www.astuteanalytica.com/industry-report/gcc-hr-tech-market

text_boxes.text: PESTEL Analysis
Political, Economic, Social, Technological, Environmental, Legal

• Saudi Nitaqat 2026–2028: 340,000+ jobs localised; sector quotas (tourism 40%, engineering 30%, marketing 60%)
• UAE Emiratisation: 8% (2025), 10% (2026); AED 108,000 per missing Emirati
• Kuwait Kuwaitisation, Oman Omanisation: Similar mandates
• Non-compliance: visa freezes, licence suspension, government contract exclusion

Product implication: OOB nationalization tracking as native dimensions with application capture and compliance dashboards.

speaker_notes:
• Mondaq, Envoy Global sources.

References:
• https://www.mondaq.com/saudiarabia/contracts-and-commercial-law/1754286

text_boxes.text:
• GCC HR Tech: US$2.6B → US$5.5B by 2032 (9.05% CAGR)
• 70% of GCC companies investing in HR tech
• Recruitment/ATS key segment
• Enterprise software consolidation in Saudi/UAE

Product implication: GCC is high-growth market; nationalization and recruiting capabilities are differentiators.

speaker_notes:
• Astute Analytica.

References:
• https://www.astuteanalytica.com/industry-report/gcc-hr-tech-market

text_boxes.text:
• WhatsApp: 98% open rate vs 20% email; 30M+ users in Saudi; dominant for recruitment
• GCC smartphone penetration: Saudi 92%, UAE 97% (highest in MENA)
• P2: 40%+ career site traffic from mobile in Middle East
• Arabic/English bilingual: mix for professional vs blue-collar roles

Product implication: WhatsApp integration; mobile-optimised apply flow; Arabic RTL support.

speaker_notes:
• Statista, Wuseller.

References:
• https://www.statista.com/statistics/1190185/mena-smartphone-penetration-rate-forecast-by-region/

text_boxes.text:
• Qiwa, GOSI, Mudad: Saudi government portals for work permits, Nitaqat dashboard
• 70% GCC companies investing in HR tech; HiredScore, Paradox integrations
• AI-powered recruitment platforms and workforce analytics

Product implication: Government portal integrations; AI-assisted screening and scheduling.

speaker_notes:
• Qiwa platform, Astute Analytica.

References:
• https://safwahr.com/qiwa-platform-complete-employer-guide-saudi-arabia-2026/

text_boxes.text:
• DATA GAP: No material recruiting-specific environmental signals
• UAE Net Zero 2050, Saudi 2060 are macro
• ESG workforce reporting may emerge

speaker_notes:
• Environmental factor has limited recruiting relevance.

References:
• N/A

text_boxes.text:
• Saudi PDPL 2023: Effective Sept 2023; SDAIA oversight; consent, breach 72h, DPO, DPIA
• KSA Interview Regulations (May 2025): 3-day notice; panel ≥2 Saudi nationals (1 HR specialist); non-Saudi ≤50%; 30-day outcome notification
• UAE PDPA 2022: Data protection requirements

Product implication: Interview scheduling must enforce 3-day notice, panel composition validation; consent capture; PDPL-compliant candidate data handling.

speaker_notes:
• Mondaq, Lexology, DLA Piper.

References:
• https://www.mondaq.com/saudiarabia/privacy-protection/1704024
• https://www.lexology.com/library/detail.aspx?g=c7de6aaf-c00f-40ea-bb2e-c2c11da83e07

text_boxes.text: Competitive Landscape
Regional specialists vs global platforms

Global platforms: Workday, SAP SuccessFactors, Oracle HCM/Taleo, ADP
Regional specialists: ZenHR (ZenATS), Talentera (Bayt.com), Darwinbox
Differentiators: ZenHR/Talentera offer native nationalisation tracking, Arabic localisation, government portal awareness
Workday wins: Enterprise HCM integration
Workday loses: GCC-specific compliance OOB

speaker_notes:
• ZenHR built for MENA; Talentera Bayt.com.

References:
• https://blog.zenhr.com/en/the-complete-hr-stack-for-multi-entity-gcc-groups-2026-executive-guide

text_boxes.text: Win/Loss Overview
GCC-explicit gaps

rows: GCC populations cannot use WD integrations with Outlook for interview scheduling or MS Teams HiredScore
CareerPlug integration; prospect may post jobs in competitor

speaker_notes:
• 2 GCC-explicit gaps from filtered dataset.

References:
• research/raw-data/filtered_gcc_opps.csv

text_boxes.text: Primary Research
Interview participants

P1 – Cyber Security & Campus Hiring Lead, Accenture (Egypt, GCC, Africa)
P2 – Performance & Innovation Manager, Baker Hughes (Global TA)
P3 – Product Owner, Talent & Resourcing, Shell (Global, franchise countries including GCC)

speaker_notes:
• Anonymised as P1, P2, P3 per research protocol.

References:
• research/GCC/customer-transcripts/

text_boxes.text:
• Nationalization: 20% Emiratisation, 60% Saudization, 50% Kuwaitisation; need to track throughout Workday
• Interview scheduling: 'If we can schedule interviews and send notifications... a lot better'; KSA 3-day notice, 50% Saudi panel
• Offer rigidity: 2-month dev cycles; 'near impossible' for config changes
• WhatsApp: 'Absolute necessary... that's how I reach out for quick closures'
• Dashboards: 'Couldn't read them'; download and create own

speaker_notes:
• P1 leads cyber security and campus hiring for GCC.

References:
• Interview_P1_Ammad_Alsairafi_Accenture.txt

text_boxes.text:
• Candidate grid: 'Navigate through different tabs... can it be more seamless?'
• Boolean search: 'Not that strong'; AI-assisted searches desired
• Interview scheduling: 'Felt more complicated than Outlook'; Paradox 'good if integrated'
• Nationalization: 'Nitaqat key mandate'; custom fields; 'we get penalties'
• WhatsApp: 'Helpful especially in markets like GCC and Saudi'
• Career site: Cannot be branded; apply redirect clunky
• Mobile: 40%+ traffic from handheld

speaker_notes:
• P2 is functional owner for Workday Recruiting.

References:
• Interview_P2_Mahboob_Khan_Baker_Hughes.mp4.txt

text_boxes.text:
• Reporting: 'Dashboard capabilities of Workday not able to accommodate'; built PowerBI
• Arabic/RTL: Workday Docs 'squares rather than actual characters' for Arabic offers
• Franchise model: GCC countries 'different operating model'; fewer openings; manual reporting
• HiredScore: 'Looking at' for high volume, low openings
• Communication: Shell policy restricts WhatsApp; email, text, MS Teams

speaker_notes:
• P3 manages talent and resourcing globally; GCC in franchise group.

References:
• Interview_P3_Arika_Yamahata_Shell.txt

text_boxes.text: Thematic Analysis
Validated themes and triangulation

1. Nationalization & Compliance: OOB gap; Nitaqat, Emiratisation, Kuwaitisation; penalties; custom-field workarounds
2. Reporting & Dashboards: PowerBI/Excel workarounds; multi-tab navigation; native dashboards insufficient
3. Interview Scheduling: GCC cannot use Outlook/MS Teams; Paradox opportunity; KSA 3-day notice, 50% Saudi panel

speaker_notes:
• Themes 1–3 are Priority 1 blockers.

References:
• 2026-03-19-GCC-PMF-Analysis-v36.md

text_boxes.text:
4. Offer Generation Rigidity: 2-month config cycles; Arabic/RTL squares; candidate document upload missing
5. WhatsApp / Candidate Comms: P1 'absolute necessary'; P2 'helpful'; 90%+ open rates; 40% hiring time reduction
6. Candidate Grid & Search: Multi-tab navigation; weak boolean search; AI-assisted matching desired

speaker_notes:
• Themes 4–6 are Priority 2 opportunities.

References:
• 2026-03-19-GCC-PMF-Analysis-v36.md

• Triangulation: P1, P2 strongest on Nationalization and Interview Scheduling.

References:
• Braun & Clarke triangulation

text_boxes.text: Roadmap Recommendations
Priority 1 and Priority 2

Problem: No OOB nationalization tracking for GCC; custom fields; penalties for non-compliance
Evidence: P1, P2; Nitaqat 2026–2028 (340,000 jobs); Emiratisation AED 108,000
Recommendation: OOB nationalization fields for Saudi (Nitaqat), UAE (Emiratisation), Kuwait (Kuwaitisation) with application capture, reporting, compliance dashboards
Why now: Nitaqat Phase April 2026; Emiratisation 10% by Dec 2026
Success: Reduced custom config; compliance reporting in-system

speaker_notes:
• #1 for E2E pipeline.

References:
• Mondaq Nitaqat; UAE Emiratisation 2026

text_boxes.text:
Problem: Native dashboards insufficient; PowerBI/Excel workarounds
Evidence: P1, P2, P3
Recommendation: Improve recruiter and leadership dashboards; reduce PowerBI dependency
Why now: Workaround fatigue; data governance risk
Success: Fewer exports; in-system dashboards meet needs

speaker_notes:
• All three customers cited.

References:
• Customer transcripts

text_boxes.text:
Problem: GCC cannot use Outlook/MS Teams; KSA 3-day notice, 50% Saudi panel
Evidence: P1, P2; CSV PG-00009165; KSA regulations May 2025
Recommendation: Integrate Paradox with GCC compliance (KSA panel rules, 3-day notice validation, consent capture)
Why now: Paradox acquired; KSA regulations effective
Success: Single-system scheduling; KSA compliance enforced

speaker_notes:
• Paradox integration + GCC compliance.

References:
• Lexology KSA Interview Regulations

text_boxes.text:
Problem: 2-month config cycles; Arabic/RTL squares; no candidate document upload
Evidence: P1, P3
Recommendation: Faster config cycles; candidate document upload; Arabic/RTL support validation
Why now: Arabic offer letters blocker; document collection offline
Success: Config agility; Arabic renders correctly; documents in-system

speaker_notes:
• P3: Workday Docs squares for Arabic.

References:
• Customer transcripts

text_boxes.text:
Problem: Email-only campaigns; WhatsApp dominant in GCC
Evidence: P1 'absolute necessary'; P2 'helpful'; 98% open rate vs 20% email
Recommendation: GA WhatsApp for GCC; extend campaigns beyond email
Why now: Early adopters; GCC highest WhatsApp penetration
Success: Faster candidate response; reduced no-shows

speaker_notes:
• P1: 'Absolute necessary'.

References:
• Wuseller WhatsApp recruitment; Statista

text_boxes.text:
Problem: Multi-tab navigation; weak boolean search; no AI-assisted matching
Evidence: P2, P3
Recommendation: Improve candidate grid (single-view); strengthen boolean search; AI-assisted matching from talent pool
Why now: HiredScore integrated; Paradox; AI adoption
Success: Single-view critical info; semantic search; matching candidates surfaced

speaker_notes:
• P2: 2M candidates, 20 in req — matching desired.

References:
• Customer transcripts

0.text: E2E Handoff: 6 Recommendations for PM Selection

text_boxes.text:
1. Nationalization & Compliance
2. Reporting & Dashboards
3. Interview Scheduling
4. Offer Generation Rigidity
5. WhatsApp Integration
6. Candidate Grid & Search

Default: Proceed with #1 if no selection.

speaker_notes:
• Present to PM for HITL selection before PRD creation.

References:
• E2E Handoff table in report
